title: Creating and Deleting Files
intro: Now that you can navigate the file system, it's time to start building things. You'll learn to create folders, create files, and yes -- delete them too. But be careful: the terminal doesn't have a trash can.

teach.title: mkdir/touch = Building Rooms and Placing Furniture
teach.body: Think of `mkdir` as **building a new room** in your house. It creates an empty space (folder) where you can put things. `touch` is like **placing a piece of furniture** in a room -- it creates a new, empty file. First you build the room (`mkdir components`), then you place furniture inside it (`touch components/Button.js`). You can build rooms inside rooms, and put furniture anywhere.

teach.title: rm = The Paper Shredder (Permanent!)
teach.body: When you delete a file with the GUI, it goes to the Trash and you can recover it. But `rm` in the terminal is like a **paper shredder** -- once a file goes through, it's gone forever. There's no Trash, no undo, no "Are you sure?" popup. This makes `rm` powerful but dangerous. Always double-check the file name before you hit enter.

teach.title: The Creation Commands
teach.body:
- `mkdir foldername` -- Create a new empty folder ("make directory")
- `mkdir -p path/to/nested` -- Create nested folders all at once
- `touch filename` -- Create a new empty file
- `touch file1 file2 file3` -- Create multiple files at once

teach.title: The Deletion Commands
teach.body:
- `rm filename` -- Delete a file permanently
- `rm -r foldername` -- Delete a folder and everything inside it
- `rmdir foldername` -- Delete an empty folder only (safer)

**Warning:** `rm` does NOT move files to the Trash. They are gone forever. Always use `ls` first to verify you're deleting the right thing.

teach.title: Creating a Project Structure
teach.code:
mkdir my-app                    # Create project folder
cd my-app                       # Move into it
mkdir src                       # Create source folder
touch src/index.js              # Create main file
touch README.md                 # Create readme
ls                              # Verify: src  README.md
teach.annotation: This is a common pattern -- create a folder, move into it, then build out the structure inside.

teach.body: Every app you build with AI tools starts with creating a project structure. When a client needs a new feature folder, a config file, or a clean project scaffold, doing it in the terminal takes seconds instead of minutes of right-clicking. Vibecoders who set up projects fast start earning money faster.
Use `mkdir` to create folders, `touch` to create files, `rm` to delete files, and `rmdir` to delete empty folders. Remember: `rm` is permanent -- there's no undo.

steps.instruction: Create a new folder called 'my-app' for your project.
steps.hint: The command to make a directory is 'mkdir' followed by the folder name.

steps.instruction: Now create an empty file called 'index.html' inside the my-app folder. You can do this without moving into it.
steps.hint: Use 'touch' followed by the path: foldername/filename.

steps.instruction: The old-notes.txt file is no longer needed. Delete it permanently.
steps.hint: Use 'rm' followed by the filename to remove it. Remember, this is permanent!

steps.instruction: Verify your work by listing the contents of the current directory.
steps.hint: Use the list command to see what's in the current folder.

exercise.completionMessage: You just created a project folder, added a file to it, and cleaned up an old file -- all without touching the mouse. This is exactly how developers scaffold new projects every day.
